hints.text: **Low-cam** (camera looks down, road looks wide) — only other always-low-cam = Switzerland (blurred there)
Drives **left**; writing = kanji + hiragana (loopy, soft) + katakana (geometric, loan words)
Plates: short white w/ **green text**; Kei cars = **yellow w/ black text**
Round concrete poles w/ screw-like extensions + info plates (region key); road lines **never yellow on the outside**
Prefecture roads = **hexagonal shields** (kanji under the number); distinctive red inverted-triangle stop sign; traffic mirrors; rural retaining walls
Learn place-name kanji: 県 (prefecture), 市 (city), 北/南/東/西 (N/S/E/W) → fast city finding
4 main islands N→S: Hokkaidō, Honshū, Shikoku, Kyūshū; 47 prefectures
Pole plates, reflectors, guy-wires & transformer mounting styles differ per region (Hokkaidō, Tōhoku, Kantō, Kansai…) — strong pinpoint tools
Orange arrow above top pole plate → Chūgoku; orange/black pole bands → Shikoku
Hokkaidō = wide flat grid farmland, snow; subtropical dense green → Kyūshū/Okinawa
Bollards may have the city/prefecture written in kanji → near-pinpoint
